entities.label: Thomas Arnold, Select English Works of John Wyclif, volume III (1871)

physicalDescription.extent: title and preliminary matter through p. xxiii; 545 Arabic-numbered pages
notes: The complete series was issued in three volumes in 1869–1871; the present cataloging subject and source are volume III.

rights.basis: The edition was published in 1871, and the U-M derived item identifies its source material as public domain in the United States.
rights.component: Arnold's 1871 editorial text, arrangement, notes, and reference matter

rights.basis: The underlying texts are medieval; this assessment is separate from Arnold's editorial layer and from modern digital reproductions.
rights.component: Medieval texts reproduced within the edition

access.accessMethod: Advance research appointment or image request
notes: No exact current online catalog or complete facsimile was found.

entities.description: Arnold's siglum U, consulted for portions of no. I.

holding.notes: The official finding-aid page says the improved Western-manuscript catalog remains in preparation; no exact current public item record or complete facsimile was found.

entities.label: Magdalen College, Oxford, MS 52

entities.description: Arnold's siglum V and base witness for nos. II, III, and V.
entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Bodley 789

entities.description: Arnold's siglum W and principal source for nos. IX, XIX, XX, XXIV, XXV, XXXI, and XXXII.
entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Bodley 647

entities.description: Arnold's siglum X and the most extensively used base witness in volume III, supplying or supporting nos. XIII, XIV, XV, XVII, XVIII, XXII, XXIV, XXVIII, and XXXIII.
entities.label: Corpus Christi College, Cambridge, MS 296

notes: Parker describes a multi-author collection once wholly attributed to Wyclif.

entities.description: Arnold's siglum Y, base witness for nos. VI–VIII and a parallel witness for nos. III and V.

holding.notes: The holding's medieval manuscripts are available on commercial microfilm; no exact current public complete digital object was found.

entities.label: Lambeth Palace Library, MS 408

entities.description: Arnold's siglum Z and base witness for no. XXVII.
entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Douce 274

entities.description: A 15th-century Wycliffite compilation used as base for nos. XII and XXI and as a parallel or incomplete witness for several CCCC 296 texts.

entities.description: Arnold's siglum BB, unique base witness for no. XXIX and a parallel witness for no. IX.

entities.description: A Wycliffite compilation used as base for no. XXVI and collated for nos. III, V, IX, X, XXIII, XXVII, and XXVIII.

entities.label: Trinity College Dublin, MS 245

access.accessMethod: Exact BL Archives and Manuscripts record; consultation or reproduction by request
notes: No public complete page-image object was found.

dateStatements.label: current catalog date
dateStatements.value: early first quarter of the 15th century

entities.description: Arnold's siglum DD; no. IV occupies ff. 166v–174r and reached Arnold through a transcript made by the person Arnold names only as Mr Brock.
entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 2398

notes: The no. IV Pater Noster is ff. 166v–174r; the same codex also contains a Short Rule at ff. 188v–190v, but Arnold bases no. XV elsewhere.

access.accessMethod: The edition records the transcript's use but supplies no holding or later history.
access.entityLabel: Mr Brock's intermediary transcript of the Pater Noster in Harley MS 2398
notes: Survival and present location remain unverified; this is distinct from access to Harley MS 2398 itself.

entities.description: The otherwise untraced working transcript from which Arnold printed no. IV. Arnold explicitly says that he did not inspect Harley MS 2398 and identifies the transcriber only as Mr Brock.
entities.label: Mr Brock's intermediary transcript of the Pater Noster in Harley MS 2398
notes: Typed as a manuscript witness because the schema has no dedicated intermediary-transcript entity type; this modern editorial transcript is not the medieval Harley codex.

entities.description: The working transcript on which Arnold says he founded nos. VI–VIII; he explicitly states that he did not see Lambeth MS 408.
entities.label: Unnamed transcript of Lambeth Palace Library MS 408 for nos. VI–VIII

entities.description: A careful working transcript of no. XIV from CCCC MS 296, revised against the codex by W. Aldis Wright before Arnold used it.
entities.label: W. Aldis Wright-revised transcript of CCCC MS 296 for no. XIV

entities.description: The careful transcript made for the Oxford University Press Delegates from TCD MS 244 (then C.III.12) and used by Arnold for no. XXI.
entities.label: Mr T. French's transcript of TCD MS 244 for no. XXI

entities.description: The working transcript on which Arnold based no. XXII, examined against CCCC MS 296 by W. Aldis Wright before it was sent to him.
entities.label: Cambridge transcript of CCCC MS 296 for no. XXII

entities.description: A roughly eighteenth-century transcript of the CCCC MS 296 tract made for John Lewis, reported by Arnold among the Rawlinsonian manuscripts and used to compare his Cambridge transcript.
entities.label: Older transcript of the no. XXII tract made for John Lewis
notes: Arnold reports its historical Bodleian location but supplies no shelfmark; a current exact catalog record has not been established.

entities.description: The transcript made for the Oxford University Press Delegates from TCD MS 245 (then C.V.6) and used by Arnold for no. XXVI.
entities.label: Mr T. French's transcript of TCD MS 245 for no. XXVI

entities.description: The working transcript from CCCC MS 296 on which Arnold says he founded no. XXVIII.
entities.label: Unnamed transcript of CCCC MS 296 for no. XXVIII

entities.description: The working transcript of the no. XXIII tract in MS Bodley 788 made for Arnold's edition.
entities.label: Unnamed transcript of MS Bodley 788 for no. XXIII

entities.description: The working collation supplied to Arnold for no. IV, comparing the manuscript now John Rylands English MS 90 with Harley MS 2398.
entities.label: Frederick Furnivall's collation of the Corser manuscript against Harley MS 2398

dateStatements.label: current catalog date
dateStatements.value: end of the 14th or beginning of the 15th century

entities.description: The manuscript Arnold knew as Mr Corser's newly discovered codex. Its Pater Noster on ff. 63r–65v was collated by Furnivall against Harley MS 2398 for no. IV.
entities.label: John Rylands Library, English MS 90

physicalDescription.format: Codex containing the Pricke of Conscience and a Pater Noster exposition

entities.description: Arnold's siglum FF and base witness for no. XV; it also preserves portions of no. I.
entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Laud Misc. 174

access.accessMethod: Exact BL Archives and Manuscripts record; consultation or reproduction by request
notes: No public complete page-image object was found.

entities.description: Arnold's siglum GG and a parallel witness for nos. III, V, and XXVII.
entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 2385

notes: Relevant first-part texts include a Pater Noster, Seven Heresies, and Ave Maria on ff. 2r–5v.

access.accessMethod: Exact BL Archives and Manuscripts record; consultation or reproduction by request
access.entityLabel: British Library, Cotton MS Tiberius C VII
notes: No public complete page-image object was found; the codex is fire damaged.

entities.description: Arnold's siglum HH, a Knighton Chronicle witness consulted for the eucharistic material in nos. XXX–XXXI; the relevant modern comparison locus is ff. 183v–184v.
entities.label: British Library, Cotton MS Tiberius C VII

notes: Damaged in the 1731 Cotton Library fire.

entities.description: Arnold's siglum II, another Knighton Chronicle witness for nos. XXX–XXXI; the relevant modern comparison locus is ff. 275v–276r.
entities.label: British Library, Cotton MS Claudius E III

notes: The Knighton Chronicle component is ff. 169r–307r; the relevant eucharistic variants are ff. 275v–276r.
No complete public page-image object was found.

entities.description: A Wycliffite sermon compilation serving as base for nos. X, XI, and XVI and as a partial comparison witness for nos. I and XXXII.

holding.notes: The official narrative collection page confirms a collection of Wycliffite sermons; no exact item record or complete public facsimile was found.

locators: part title leaves and printed pp. 1–545

access.entityLabel: Trinity College Dublin digital object for MS 244
notes: Current reCAPTCHA bot mitigation blocked automated manifest retrieval. Canvas count, exact tract loci, and full physical-object coverage remain unverified; no local cache was made.
access.provider: The Library of Trinity College Dublin

rights.basis: TCD's current open-licensing policy generally places digital surrogates of out-of-copyright collection items under CC BY 4.0 but identifies exceptions and asks users to clarify the license or publication permission; the exact MS 244 item license could not be inspected through current bot mitigation.
rights.component: TCD manuscript photography and Digital Collections delivery
notes: Do not treat the general policy as an item-level license confirmation until the MS 244 object metadata is inspected or TCD responds.

entities.description: The official digitised-manuscript list links a public Digital Collections object for MS 244. Current bot mitigation prevented retrieval of the manifest, canvas count, folio mapping, and exact physical-object coverage, so completeness is not asserted.
entities.label: Trinity College Dublin digital object for MS 244

rights.basis: TCD's current open-licensing policy generally places digital surrogates of out-of-copyright collection items under CC BY 4.0 but identifies exceptions and asks users to clarify the license or publication permission; the exact MS 244 item license could not be inspected through current bot mitigation.
rights.component: TCD manuscript photography and Digital Collections delivery
notes: Do not treat the general policy as an item-level license confirmation until the MS 244 object metadata is inspected or TCD responds.

next_steps: Review each tract against Anne Hudson's bibliography and later critical editions; retain Arnold's umbrella attribution only as disputed historical evidence until that review is complete.
openQuestions.question: What current specialist attribution, date, and textual-layer model applies to each numbered selection and to the Rolle/Wycliffite layers of no. I?

next_steps: Compare modern CCCC 296 tract handlists with Arnold's no. XXIII and XXVIII headnotes; preserve Bodley 788 as the directly stated base for XXIII meanwhile.
openQuestions.question: Is Arnold's front-list assignment of no. XXIII to CCCC MS 296 a typographical error for no. XXVIII?

next_steps: Trace the Brock, Lambeth, Wright-revised, French, Cambridge, Lewis/Rawlinson, Bodley 788, CCCC 296, and Furnivall materials through Oxford University Press Delegate records, Arnold papers, collaborator correspondence, and repository catalogs; preserve surname- or initials-only identities unless documentary evidence resolves them.
openQuestions.question: Which of the working transcripts and Furnivall collation used for Arnold's volume survive, and where are they now held?

next_steps: Use an interactive browser or ask TCD Digital Collections for the IIIF manifest, canvas-to-folio map, statement about boards/flyleaves/blanks/exteriors, and exact item license; cache only if the verified terms permit whole-object retrieval.
notes: Current reCAPTCHA bot mitigation blocked unattended retrieval; failed automation is not treated as evidence that the object is unavailable to users. No repository enquiry has yet been sent.
openQuestions.question: What are TCD MS 244's canvas inventory, physical-object coverage, exact CME tract loci, and item-level reuse terms?

next_steps: Use the exact catalog and repository enquiry routes to request whole-object availability, image counts, coverage of covers/spine/pastedowns/flyleaves/blanks, cost, and reuse terms; do not treat selected images or commercial microfilm as complete without an inventory audit.
notes: No repository enquiry has yet been sent.
openQuestions.question: Can authorized complete digital copies be obtained for the manuscript witnesses that lack a complete public image object, especially Bodley 288, Bodley 789, Bodley 647, Lambeth 408, TCD MS 245, Harley 2398, Harley 2385, Cotton Tiberius C VII, New College MS 95, and Magdalen MS 52?

next_steps: Request written clarification or an authorized bundle through Manchester's rights/reproduction route; do not bulk-extract the 152-canvas public object under current systematic-extraction terms.
notes: No permission enquiry has yet been sent.
openQuestions.question: Will Manchester authorize a whole-object research cache or supply an institutionally approved complete reproduction of John Rylands English MS 90?

next_steps: Ask U-M Digital Content and Collections for item-level selection, keying, proofreading, and source-copy notes while preserving the directly observed mapping.
openQuestions.question: What U-M production record explains retention of the title page and pp. xiii–545 while omitting Arnold's introduction on pp. v–xi?

reviewNotes: Arnold's umbrella Wyclif attribution is a historical edition claim, not a modern whole-volume authorship conclusion.

A base-text chain means Arnold states or directly implies that the printed tract ultimately derives from the named codex; explicit working transcripts sit between edition and codex, while a collation relation records comparison only.
Complete facsimile coverage is always qualified relative to the provider's exposed inventory; absent boards, spine, pastedowns, or unexposed leaves remain disclosed.

Working transcripts and a supplied collation are typed manuscript_witness because schema version 1.0.0 has no intermediary-transcript or collation-artifact entity type; labels and relation chains keep them distinct from medieval codices.

lineage.summary: CME00031 is the repository XML for a University of Michigan manually keyed and proofread electronic text derived from selected matter in Thomas Arnold's 1871 third volume of Select English Works of John Wyclif. The encoding retains the edition title page, printed pp. xiii–xxiii, all thirty-three numbered selections on pp. 1–523, and the appendix, glossary, and index through p. 545; it omits Arnold's introduction on pp. v–xi and copy-specific physical matter. Arnold's edition ultimately draws on twenty-four identified medieval manuscript codices, sometimes through separately modeled working transcripts or Furnivall's supplied collation rather than Arnold's direct inspection. The record distinguishes base witnesses, material intermediaries, and collated witnesses and preserves Arnold's own attribution doubts, including the demonstrably post-Wyclif no. XXIX. Complete exposed digital objects are cached for the 1871 volume, CCCC MS 296, and Cotton Claudius E III; three Digital Bodleian caches are explicitly partial and do not overlap the cited CME passages. John Rylands English MS 90 and TCD MS 244 have public manuscript viewers but are not locally cached: Manchester prohibits systematic extraction of substantial parts, while TCD's bot mitigation prevented a reliable manifest and physical-coverage audit.

catalogSummary.author: John Wyclif, traditionally attributed; Arnold identifies Rolle-derived material and anonymous Wycliffite or Lollard writing, and several attributions are disputed or rejected
catalogSummary.date: Individual medieval composition dates remain under review; no. XXIX dates to 1388–1389; edition published 1871
catalogSummary.title: Select English Works of John Wyclif, Volume III: Miscellaneous Works

catalogingSubject.label: Select English Works of John Wyclif, Volume III: Miscellaneous Works
catalogingSubject.scope_note: The edition-level compilation represented by University of Michigan CME item CME00031: Thomas Arnold's 1871 third volume, which selects and edits thirty-three Middle English religious and polemical texts then ascribed to John Wyclif, together with encoded title matter, manuscript and bibliographic lists, contents, editorial headnotes, appendix, glossary, and a three-volume index. This record does not collapse the compilation, its constituent medieval works, their disputed and multiple authorships, the physical manuscripts, Arnold's complete printed volume, the U-M encoding, or the repository XML into one abstract medieval work.

contentParts.label: Encoded title and front matter
contentParts.summary: Edition title page; list of manuscripts; bibliography of Wyclif literature; classified list of English works ascribed to Wyclif; and contents.

contentParts.label: Part I. Exegetical and Didactic Treatises
contentParts.summary: Fifteen numbered selections on canticles, basic prayers and doctrine, senses, sins, mercy, love, scripture, marriage, clerical support, and rules of life.

contentParts.label: I. Super Cantica Sacra
contentParts.summary: Commentaries on twelve biblical canticles, combining a Rolle-derived core with Wycliffite or Lollard revision and additions.

contentParts.label: II. Þe Ten Comaundementis
contentParts.summary: Exposition of the Ten Commandments.

contentParts.label: III. The Pater Noster
contentParts.summary: A short exposition of the Lord's Prayer.

contentParts.label: IV. Þe Pater Noster
contentParts.summary: A longer vernacular exposition of the Lord's Prayer, printed from a transcript of Harley MS 2398 and collated with the Corser manuscript, now John Rylands English MS 90.

contentParts.label: V. Ave Maria
contentParts.summary: Exposition of the Ave Maria.

contentParts.label: VI. On the Apostles' Creed
contentParts.summary: A brief exposition of the Apostles' Creed.

contentParts.label: VII. On the Five Outer Wits
contentParts.summary: A brief moral exposition of the five external senses.

contentParts.label: VIII. On the Five Inner Wits
contentParts.summary: A brief moral exposition of the five internal senses.

contentParts.label: IX. On the Seven Deadly Sins
contentParts.summary: An extended moral treatment of the seven deadly sins.

contentParts.label: X. Þe Seven Werkys of Mercy Bodyly and Gostly
contentParts.summary: Companion expositions of the seven corporal and seven spiritual works of mercy, encoded in two consecutive divisions under one numeral.

contentParts.label: XI. Five Questions on Love
contentParts.summary: Five questions concerning Christian love.

contentParts.label: XII. On the Sufficiency of Holy Scripture
contentParts.summary: A short argument for the sufficiency and authority of scripture.

contentParts.label: XIII. Of Weddid Men and Wifis and of Here Children Also
contentParts.summary: Pastoral instruction on marriage, spouses, and children.

contentParts.label: XIV. De Stipendiis Ministrorum
contentParts.summary: On how priests should be supported.

contentParts.label: XV. A Schort Reule of Lif
contentParts.summary: A short rule of life for people generally and for priests, lords, and laborers in particular.

contentParts.label: Part II. Controversial Works
contentParts.summary: Fourteen polemical selections on simony, clerical property and conduct, prayer, schism, excommunication, friars, apostasy, heresy, and Lollard defenses.

contentParts.label: XVI. Simonists and Apostates
contentParts.summary: A short polemic against simonists and apostates.

contentParts.label: XVII. Church Temporalities
contentParts.summary: On the church's temporal property and lordship.

contentParts.label: XVIII. De Precationibus Sacris
contentParts.summary: A treatise on sacred prayer.

contentParts.label: XIX. Lincolniensis
contentParts.summary: A short anti-fraternal tract framed by an attribution to Robert Grosseteste, bishop of Lincoln.

contentParts.label: XX. Vita Sacerdotum
contentParts.summary: A polemic on the proper life of priests.

contentParts.label: XXI. De Pontificum Romanorum Schismate
contentParts.summary: On the papal schism.

contentParts.label: XXII. The Grete Sentence of Curs Expouned
contentParts.summary: An extended exposition and critique of the great sentence of excommunication.

contentParts.label: XXIII. The Church and Her Members
contentParts.summary: A treatise on the church and its members.

contentParts.label: XXIV. Fifty Heresies and Errors of Friars
contentParts.summary: A numbered indictment of fifty alleged errors of the friars.

contentParts.label: XXV. De Blasphemia, Contra Fratres
contentParts.summary: A polemic against the friars concerning blasphemy.

contentParts.label: XXVI. De Apostasia Cleri
contentParts.summary: A polemic on clerical apostasy.

contentParts.label: XXVII. Seven Heresies
contentParts.summary: A treatment of seven alleged heresies.

contentParts.label: XXVIII. Octo in Quibus Seducuntur Simplices Christiani
contentParts.summary: Eight ways in which simple Christians are said to be deceived.

contentParts.label: XXIX. On the Twenty-Five Articles
contentParts.summary: A Lollard response to twenty-five charges, composed in 1388 or 1389 and therefore not by Wyclif.

contentParts.label: Part III. Letters and Documents
contentParts.summary: Four short eucharistic, epistolary, and parliamentary texts.

contentParts.label: XXX. Concerning the Eucharist
contentParts.summary: An English eucharistic confession transmitted in Knighton's Chronicle; Arnold prints it as connected with Wyclif, while later comparison treats its wording and responsibility cautiously.

contentParts.label: XXXI. Concerning the Eucharist
contentParts.summary: A second English eucharistic confession, edited from Bodley 647 and compared with Knighton's Chronicle.

contentParts.label: XXXII. Letter to Pope Urban
contentParts.summary: An English version or amplification of Wyclif's 1384 Latin letter to Pope Urban VI.

contentParts.label: XXXIII. A Petition to the King and Parliament
contentParts.summary: A Wycliffite petition arranged in four articles and addressed to the king and parliament.

contentParts.label: Appendix: On the Authenticity of the Fifth Book of Knyghton's Chronicle
contentParts.summary: Arnold's note on the authenticity and manuscript basis of the fifth book of Knighton's Chronicle.

contentParts.label: Glossary
contentParts.summary: Glossary to the Middle English texts.

contentParts.label: Index to Wyclif's Sermons and Miscellaneous Works
contentParts.summary: Combined index to the sermons and miscellaneous works across Arnold's three-volume edition.

dateStatements.display: Arnold's third volume was published in 1871
notes: The three-volume series spans 1869–1871; 1871 is the title-page date of volume III and the date of this edition-level cataloging subject.

dateStatements.display: No. XXIX, On the Twenty-Five Articles, was composed in 1388 or 1389
notes: Arnold derives the terminus from Knighton's 1388 list and the reference to Pope Urban VI, who died in 1389; the date excludes Wyclif, who died in 1384, as author.

extent.description: Thirty-three numbered medieval selections in three parts, plus title matter, four front-matter lists or contents units, appendix, glossary, and index. The XML contains 11 DIV1, 38 DIV2, 219 DIV3, 1,201 P, 1,380 NOTE, 561 PB, 6 LG, and 41 L elements. Only one LG with three L children belongs directly to a medieval selection (no. XI); four LG and 31 L are inside NOTE, and one LG and seven L are inside HEADNOTE. No. X occupies two consecutive DIV2 units under one Roman numeral; nos. VI–VIII have an editorial wrapper followed by distinct divisions.

formStatements.description: Prose compilation. No. XI alone contains one direct verse group of three lines; the XML's other five LG and thirty-eight L elements occur in editorial NOTE or HEADNOTE matter and do not make their numbered works mixed-form.

scope.description: Editorial English occurs in title matter, lists, contents, headnotes, appendix, glossary, and index throughout the compilation
Latin occurs as embedded titles, quoted authorities, formulas, and passages throughout the compilation

notes: The volume title and inherited U-M author field are recorded as historical attribution evidence, not accepted as proof that Wyclif authored every medieval selection.
The record distinguishes medieval composition, physical witness production, Arnold's 1871 editorial compilation, U-M's 2016–2018 encoding batch, and the repository artifact.
Element totals and verse/prose counts are representation metrics from the XML, not newly normalized critical extents.
Witness identities and Arnold's use of them are modeled in the linked lineage manifest; a manuscript used only for collation is not automatically treated as the base of the printed text.

next_steps: Review each tract against Anne Hudson's bibliography and subsequent item-level editions; retain Arnold's historical Wyclif umbrella only as a disputed attribution until that work is complete.
openQuestions.question: What is the current specialist attribution, date, and textual-layer model for each of the thirty-three numbered items?

next_steps: Inspect CCCC 296 and modern tract handlists to determine whether the front-list reference is a typo, probably for no. XXVIII; preserve Bodley 788 as Arnold's directly stated base meanwhile.
openQuestions.question: Why does Arnold's front manuscript list assign no. XXIII to CCCC MS 296 when the no. XXIII headnote instead identifies Bodley 788 and Trinity College Dublin MS 245?

next_steps: Ask U-M Digital Content and Collections for selection, keying, and proofreading notes while preserving the directly observed source mapping.
openQuestions.question: What item-level U-M production record explains retention of the title page and pp. xiii–545 while omitting Arnold's Introduction on pp. v–xi?

notes: This broad cultural origin does not transfer the production place of any one surviving witness to the composition of every text.

scope.description: The Middle English religious, polemical, and documentary texts

notes: Oxford is the title-page place of publication. This statement does not claim that all compilation or editorial labor occurred there, and it is not the composition place of every medieval item.

Arnold selected thirty-two of Shirley's sixty-five English works and part of another, organized them in three parts, supplied headnotes and reference matter, and excluded items he considered unsuitable or inauthentic.

The volume title preserves the historical attribution, but Arnold himself rejects Wyclif's authorship of no. XXIX and questions several other items; current Parker cataloging describes the principal CCCC 296 collection as multi-author.

scope.description: The thirty-three numbered medieval selections as presented by Arnold

notes: Arnold distinguishes a Rolle-derived core, Lollard retouching, and five additional canticle commentaries; this is not whole-item authorship. The exact authorial layering requires modern specialist review.

scope.description: The Richard Rolle core of the first seven canticle commentaries within no. I
Representative texts or textual layers treated as works of Wyclif's followers: the additions to no. I, posthumous no. XXIX, and the amplified vernacular material in no. XXXII

notes: Arnold states that no. IV was set from Brock's accurate transcript of Harley MS 2398; Arnold did not inspect that codex directly. The edition supplies only the surname, so this record does not silently expand it to an unverified personal identity.
Furnivall supplied Arnold with a useful collation of the Corser manuscript, now John Rylands English MS 90, against Harley MS 2398.
Arnold says Wright revised the careful CCCC MS 296 transcript used for no. XIV.
Arnold says Wright examined the Cambridge transcript against CCCC MS 296 before it was sent to him.
Arnold identifies Mr T. French as the transcriber of the TCD MS 244 and MS 245 texts used for nos. XXI and XXVI; the source does not expand his name further.

subjects.label: Church reform, clerical authority, and religious orders
Authorship, attribution, and the construction of the Wycliffite corpus

summaries.value: Arnold's third volume gathers thirty-three Middle English texts in three editorial parts: fifteen exegetical and didactic selections, fourteen controversial works, and four letters or documents. It preserves a historically important but unstable Wyclif attribution: Arnold rejects Wyclif's authorship of the post-1384 no. XXIX, recognizes Rolle material in no. I, and repeatedly qualifies other ascriptions. The encoded compilation also contains the edition title page, manuscript and Wyclif-literature lists, a classified list of works ascribed to Wyclif, contents, headnotes and notes, an appendix on Knighton's Chronicle, glossary, and combined index to all three volumes.

CME00031 encodes the edition title page, printed pp. xiii–xxiii, all three part title leaves and printed pp. 1–545 through the final index. It omits Arnold's Introduction on printed pp. v–xi and the physical scan's unprinted binding, blanks, flyleaves, library marks, and other copy-specific matter.

It preserves a historically important but unstable Wyclif attribution: Arnold rejects Wyclif's authorship of the post-1384 no. XXIX, recognizes Rolle material in no. I, and repeatedly qualifies other ascriptions. The encoded compilation also contains the edition title page, manuscript and Wyclif-literature lists, a classified list of works ascribed to Wyclif, contents, headnotes and notes, an appendix on Knighton's Chronicle, glossary, and combined index to all three volumes.
